Email #1, from Cameron (scam-baiter):

Dear Mr. <PRESIDIO_ANONYMIZED_PERSON>,

Thank you for reaching out to me amidst what must be a difficult time for you. I am deeply sorry to hear about your diagnosis and the challenges you are facing. Your courage and desire to make a positive impact on the lives of others is truly commendable.

Firstly, I want to assure you that I am open-minded and sincere in offering my assistance. It would be an honor to be a part of fulfilling your last wish on Earth. I understand the importance of trust in a situation like this, especially considering the unfortunate actions of your family members.

In order to proceed, I kindly request more information about the details of the funds you have mentioned. Once I have a better understanding of the situation, I will be able to assist you accordingly. It is crucial to ensure that every step we take is in line with your wishes and that the funds are distributed to deserving charity organizations.

To facilitate the process, please provide me with the contact information of your attorney, who will help formalize the necessary legal documents. Please also let me know which bank the money was deposited in, the amount that was deposited, and any other relevant information you believe is important for me to know.

Rest assured, I will approach this responsibility with dedication and empathy, keeping your legacy and kindheartedness at the forefront of everything I do. It is important for both of us to stay prayerful and in gratitude for the opportunities that life offers.

I eagerly await your response and further instructions so that we can move forward with your noble cause. May you find strength and peace during this challenging time. Stay blessed, and may your final days be filled with love and comfort.

Warmest regards,
Cameron McCaffrey

Email #2, from Anthony:

24/01/2024

Greetings My Dear Brother: Cameron McCaffrey,

Thank you so much dear brother Cameron, for your kind response to my request. I am immensely grateful to you and for your acceptance to help me in distributing the fund to the poor and needy ones in your country and beyond as I pleading with you. May the good thing always follow you as you do this to humanity. You will truly be bless brother for your kindness towards this my last wish. In fact, I am glad that God wanted to use you to achieve my last aim and objective before I leave this earth. I would like to inform you that I am not afraid of the challenges I am facing today as I know that everyone will die someday.

So, my greatest concern now is to see that this charity project of my last wish is fulfilled by you. Dear, brother, you may see this as an opportunity for me to take advantage of you but believe me i am truly dying here gradually with this sickness and instead of keeping quiet and die lonely leaving the entire fund with my bank. Some officials of the bank will confiscated the funds and divert it to their personal account. I rather entrust it to someone like you to help me use it for a charity work in the earth.

Frankly, my brother, I am enduring a lot of pains down my waist and all over my body has swollen a little. Honestly, I am really on a countdown and cannot have the chance to do anything with the funds again. There are a lot of things that I have been bound to do by the management of the hospital to help my condition from deteriorating more. Please try to understand me and don`t talk down on me because nobody knows tomorrow.

Therefore, I am very much appreciative and happy to get someone like you who is willing and willing to volunteer to handle this charity work and fulfill my generous wish before I depart from this world. Moreover, please note that I dully entrusted and put everything on you that you will see this project through to assist the needy and poor in the society as I proposed which I am giving you the opportunity today, Therefore, give this very charity the utmost attention it deserves as it was my last wish on earth.

Meanwhile, I would like to inform you that the amount I deposited in the bank is about £3.5Million Great British Pounds, and I would like you to use (70%) of the money to assist the needy, the poor and the less privileges ones in your country through charity organizations when you receive the money, while you take (30%) of the money as compensation for your great assistance to me in this generous task.

You are to send your full particulars to me so that I will forward it to the bank in London in order for them to start processing your data file and the fund accredited and be credited to your personal account. The particulars you are to send are: YOUR FULL NAME, YOUR FULL ADDRESS, YOUR TELEPHONE NUMBER, AND YOUR COMPLETE BANK ACCOUNT DETAILS. Do send this information to me at once so that I will forward it to my bank in London for them to process and update your database before the fund would be transferred to you.

Therefore, I would like to inform you that I do not know if there's any expenses involve in this transaction but If there's any, please try to comply as I know that it will not be much if any so as to get this transfer to success as you will never be disappointed and you will surely reap the fruits of your good labor. Also, I hope to meet you face to face here in the hospital once you receive and confirm the transfer before you commence on the charity project.

I Attached here some photographs of me in the hospital bed with some doctors assigned to always attend to me. As you can see, it was very critical that i couldn`t even stand on my own two legs. My body pains are increasing every day by day that i won`t hold myself. I don`t want to die without fulfilling this charity work with you. Please do this for me. Thanks, while looking forward to hearing from you today with the particulars. Best regards and God bless.

Yours indeed,

Your Brother
Mr. Anthony Jenkins

N/b:  Please treat this matter with urgency, as I would be glad if you can receive this money and fulfill this charity project at my present before the end of my time in this world. Thank you.

Email #3, from Cameron (scam-baiter):

Subject: Excited to Proceed with Our Charitable Endeavors

Dear Mr. <PRESIDIO_ANONYMIZED_PERSON>,

It was with a heavy heart that I read your most recent email. The strength and fortitude you possess in facing such daunting circumstances are nothing short of inspiring. The resolve with which you wish to make a difference in the world reinforces my commitment to ensuring your last wishes are realized with the utmost respect and efficiency.

Before we proceed, I hope you could elaborate on a few points to help me fully grasp the scope and logistics of your desired charity project. This information will also serve to ensure that your benevolent vision is executed precisely as you intend.

1. Could you kindly provide me with a list of preferred charities or specific sectors (such as health, education, poverty alleviation) which you wish to support through this noble endeavor?

2. It would be greatly helpful to understand why you have chosen me, specifically, for this act of kindness. Were there certain qualities or criteria you were seeking in a partner for this venture?

3. Do you have legal representation or a contact at the bank we could liaise with to facilitate transparency and seamless communication? It would be reassuring to discuss some of the legalities with a lawyer to ensure we are complying with international transfer laws.

4. You mentioned photos of you in the hospital bed accompanied this email, but I seem to have trouble viewing them. Could you please resend them in a different format or let me know if there is another way to view them?

5. Regarding the transfer of funds and potential expenses involved, I would appreciate a more detailed breakdown of the associated costs, if any, to prepare for how best to handle them. Understanding these costs is critical to ensuring the largest possible portion of funds is directed to the charities.

6. Lastly, could you share the stories or experiences that influenced your decision to dedicate such a significant portion of your assets to charity? Your journey and motivations could be instrumental in creating a narrative that encourages others to continue charitable acts in your memory.

I am ready to provide you with my details, as requested. However, for the purpose of security, may I first verify your identity and the authenticity of this transfer? This extra step will help protect both of us against any potential misunderstanding or misuse.

In the meantime, I would urge you not to worry about the urgency. Rest assured, I am wholeheartedly committed to this process and will do my utmost to expedite matters. However, thoroughness and diligence are key to ensuring your wishes are followed to the letter, which I am sure you can appreciate.

Wishing you comfort and peace during this time. I am looking forward to making a positive impact together.

Blessings and warm regards,

Cameron McCaffrey

Email #2, from Herman:

Dear Cameron,

Thanks for reaching out and your interest in participating in the Private Placement Program (PPP).
I am attaching two KYC templates which I believe Bernard has submitted to you also.
From those two attached templates, please select only one that refers to you as an Individual or as a Company to invest.

If you are the Signatory of the Company who will invest, then please use Corporate version.
If you invest under your own name as an Individual, please select the Individual template.

I recommend the KYC is dated on Monday, because the validity of KYC only lasts very quickly in 3 days only. That way, if you submit the KYC dated Friday, we are going to lose weekend days.

The KYC template has its self explanatory page by page, which I will describe to you page by page below.
All pages must be signed and initials in wet blue ink, no electronic signatures.
Let me go over the Individual KYC template as below:

Page 1:
The transaction code you can leave it blank for now. Later when your application is approved, the Trade Compliance will provide that transaction code accordingly.

Page 2: Sign on the left lower box, name/title, passport number and country of issuance.
Initial on left bottom footnote.

Page 3: Put your full name in (NAME).
Initial on left bottom footnote.

Page 4: Put your full name in (NAME).
Sign on the left lower box, name/title, passport number, date of issue, date of expiry, and country of issuance.
Initial on left bottom footnote.

Page 5: Fill out the Client Information Sheet as complete as possible.
Initial on left bottom footnote.

Page 6: Fill out Legal Advisor, if any.
Initial on left bottom footnote.

Page 7: Fill out Client Account where Profits to be Paid, or to appoint a Paymaster.
Initial on left bottom footnote.

Page 8: Declare how much would you like to invest, currency type, origin of funds, confirmation whether the funds are free and clear of all liens, encumbrances and third party interests.
Put your full name in (NAME).
Sign on the brown box.
Put name, title, passport number, date of issue, date of expiry, country of issuance underneath your signature.
Initial on left bottom footnote.

Page 9: Fill out LOI by put your full name in (NAME). Declare how much you would like to invest.
Initial in left bottom footnote.

Page 10: To sign the LOI on brown box.
Put name, title, passport number, date of issue, date of expiry, country of issuance underneath your signature.

Page 11: To fill out Letter of Cease & Desist Confirmation
Put your full name in (NAME), put country in (COUNTRY), passport number in (NUMBER).
Date has to be consistent with the date of KYC submission, which I recommend next Monday, 1/29/2024.
Sign on the brown box.
Put name, title, passport number, date of issue, date of expiry, country of issuance underneath your signature.
Initial in left bottom footnote.

Page 12: To fill out Source of Funds Affidavit.
Put your full name in (NAME), put country in (COUNTRY), passport number in (NUMBER).
Declare how much you own the funds, Bank account number (ACCOUNT NUMBER), at which Bank (NAME OF THE BANK) and its location (ADDRESS OF BANK).
Date has to be consistent with the date of KYC submission, which I recommend next Monday, 1/29/2024.
Sign on the brown box.
Put name, title, passport number, date of issue, date of expiry, country of issuance underneath your signature.
Initial in left bottom footnote.

Page 13: To sign the Source of Funds Affidavit on brown box.
Put name, title, passport number, date of issue, date of expiry, country of issuance underneath your signature.
Initial in left bottom footnote.

Page 14: To fill out Authorization to Verify Funds.
Put your full name in (NAME), fill out the investment amount (SPELL AMOUNT).
Fill out Bank details: Bank name, Bank address, Account name, Account number, Account Signatory, Bank Officer & Title.
Date has to be consistent with the date of KYC submission, which I recommend next Monday, 1/29/2024.
Sign on the brown box.
Put name, title, passport number, date of issue, date of expiry, country of issuance underneath your signature.
CC on the bottom: please put name of Bank and Bank Officer.
Initial in left bottom footnote.

Page 15: To fill out Letter of Confirmation of Bank Officer.
Date has to be consistent with the date of KYC submission, which I recommend next Monday, 1/29/2024.
Put your full name in (NAME), country (COUNTRY), Passport# (NUMBER), (NAME OF BANK), (ADDRESS OF BANK).
Fill out box of Bank Officer Name & Title; Bank Officer Phone; Account No.
Sign on the brown box.
Put name, title, passport number, date of issue, date of expiry, country of issuance underneath your signature.
Initial in left bottom footnote.

Page 16: 2 Copy of Banker's Business Card.
Please scan and attach two Business Cards belong to the Bank Officers where your funds are located, and signed by those Bank Officers.
Your Initial in left bottom footnote.

Page 17: Passport.
Please scan your passport in high resolution and attach it on this page.
Initial in left bottom footnote.

Page 18: Proof of Funds.
Fresh Bank statement dated the same as KYC submission date, signed by 2 bank officers with their titles and PIN.
The RWA (Ready, Willing, Able)/BCL (Bank Confirmation Letter) Letter from the Bank is usually needed at a later stage. However, if your Bank could provide it dated the same as KYC submission date, then it would be perfect. I am attaching the RWA/BCL template that you can discuss with your Banker to provide one.
Initial in left bottom footnote.

Page 19: Recent Tear Sheet are required.
You can ask your Banker to provide the Tear Sheet with the date of consistent with KYC date.
Tear Sheet needs to be signed by two (2) Bank Officers, with Title and PIN.
Initial in the left bottom footnote.

Page 20: Proof of Life (POL).
Buy newspaper on the same day of your KYC submission date.
Hold your passport with your photo facing the camera.
Ask someone or selfie with you are holding the paperwork above with your face is facing the camera.
Attach that photo result on this page.
Initial in the left bottom footnote.

Page 21: BCL.
Please note Page 18 above is actually for the Bank statement page.
Meanwhile you need to attach RWA/BCL from your Bank on this page.
Initial in the left bottom footnote.

Page 22: RWA.
This is basically the same document as BCL. However, when the Trade is about to start, usually the Trade Desk will require a refreshed RWA from your Bank.
Initial in the left bottom footnote.

Page 23: Letter of Liaison And Communications Authority.
Since you speak English, there is no need to appoint Liaison Officer.
You can put N/A since this page is Not Applicable to you.
Initial in the left bottom footnote.

What I suggest that you can start working on KYC preparation at your earliest convenience.
Once the draft KYC is ready, please submit it to me for further review so we can perfect it together.
I will always be available to support you the best I can.
If you need to talk to me by phone, my phone number is <PRESIDIO_ANONYMIZED_PHONE_NUMBER>.
My time zone is Pacific Time (Southern California).

All the best to you.
Regards,
<PRESIDIO_ANONYMIZED_PERSON>

Email #3, from Cameron:

Dear Herman,

Thank you for your prompt response and for providing me with the detailed instructions for the KYC process. I appreciate your thoroughness in explaining each page of the KYC template.

I have a few questions to ensure that I understand everything correctly:

1. On page 1, you mentioned leaving the transaction code blank for now. When should I expect to receive the transaction code, and how will it be provided to me?

2. Regarding the Client Information Sheet on page 5, are there any specific details or documents that I should include to ensure it is as complete as possible?

3. On page 8, when declaring how much I would like to invest, should I include any supporting documents or references for the origin of funds?

4. For the Letter of Cease & Desist Confirmation on page 11, could you please provide some examples of what I should include in the declaration? I want to ensure that I am providing the correct information.

5. On page 12, when filling out the Source of Funds Affidavit, I noticed that it asks for the address of the bank. Should I provide the physical address of the bank branch or the mailing address of the bank’s headquarters?

6. Regarding the Authorization to Verify Funds on page 14, could you please clarify what is meant by “Account Signatory”? Is this the name of the person who has authorization to access and manage the account?

7. On page 15, for the Letter of Confirmation of a Bank Officer, should I fill out the account number of the bank account where my funds are located?

8. Regarding page 18, do I need to provide a recent Bank Confirmation Letter (BCL) in addition to the fresh bank statement? Or is the fresh bank statement sufficient for now?

9. On page 20, for the Proof of Life (POL), is it acceptable to use a digital newspaper or does it specifically need to be a physical newspaper?

10. Finally, for pages 21 and 22, would it be possible to provide examples or templates of the Bank Confirmation Letter (BCL) and the Ready, Willing, and Able Letter (RWA), so that I can better understand what is expected?

I apologize for the multitude of questions, but I want to make sure that I complete the KYC process accurately and efficiently. Thank you for your patience and support.

Best regards,
Cameron McCaffrey

Email #4, from Herman:

Hi Cameron,

You are most welcome.
Let me answer your inquiries below, typed by Blue font.

On Tue, Jan 23, 2024 at 11:24 AM Cameron McCaffrey <cmccaff1@fau.edu> wrote:
Dear Herman,

Thank you for your prompt response and for providing me with the detailed instructions for the KYC process. I appreciate your thoroughness in explaining each page of the KYC template.

I have a few questions to ensure that I understand everything correctly:

1. On page 1, you mentioned leaving the transaction code blank for now. When should I expect to receive the transaction code, and how will it be provided to me?
The transaction code will be provided by the Trade Platform after they conduct the due diligence of your KYC and your application is approved. 


2. Regarding the Client Information Sheet on page 5, are there any specific details or documents that I should include to ensure it is as complete as possible?
Basically, you can just fill out the fields on the table as complete as possible, I believe you will be fine. 


3. On page 8, when declaring how much I would like to invest, should I include any supporting documents or references for the origin of funds?
There is no need to include supporting documents of the origin of funds. You can just type HONESTLY about the origin of funds. Honest is the key word, because if you don't disclose it honestly, and later the Trade Compliance finds out contrary to what you declare, your application will be rejected permanently. 


4. For the Letter of Cease & Desist Confirmation on page 11, could you please provide some examples of what I should include in the declaration? I want to ensure that I am providing the correct information.
This page is very simple, all you need to do just to fill out the (Parentheses) , put the date the same as KYC submission date, sign and initial at the bottom, and put your name, title, passport number, date of issue, date of expiry, country of issuance. That's it, no more than that.


5. On page 12, when filling out the Source of Funds Affidavit, I noticed that it asks for the address of the bank. Should I provide the physical address of the bank branch or the mailing address of the bank’s headquarters?
The Bank address should be the same as the address of the Bank where your funds are located. 


6. Regarding the Authorization to Verify Funds on page 14, could you please clarify what is meant by “Account Signatory”? Is this the name of the person who has authorization to access and manage the account?
Yes, that is right. The Private Placement Program is only applicable to the Account Signatory who has authorization to access and to manage the bank account. 


7. On page 15, for the Letter of Confirmation of a Bank Officer, should I fill out the account number of the bank account where my funds are located?
Yes Sir. 


8. Regarding page 18, do I need to provide a recent Bank Confirmation Letter (BCL) in addition to the fresh bank statement? Or is the fresh bank statement sufficient for now?
This is the requirement that most Applicants fail, because sometimes it is not easy for the Applicants to get BCL from their Bank. To increase your application chance to be accepted, I suggest that you provide the BCL in addition to the fresh bank statement. That way, your application will stand out, look more acceptable, and increase the appetite for the Trade Compliance to support you for the success. I can say this document is the key success for you as an Applicant. It is the most difficult document to obtain, but it is worth pursuing for your success. 


9. On page 20, for the Proof of Life (POL), is it acceptable to use a digital newspaper or does it specifically need to be a physical newspaper?
